I have 300 shares every month it does pay very nice dividends, but it also goes down in the value for the past 6 months in my account, which is between 10 and 20% at loss.  Initially I had close to 200 around $22 and then when the price dropped on to 17 or $18 I'll purchase another 100 shares.
( today or tomorrow is the ex-dividend date so I will be selling all my shares on the 7th) this is good for short-term but not best for long term from my opinion. I will invest my money in Vanguard dividend ETF such as vym it pays around 4% dividend and also goes up in value on 4% so you really give or take 8% better than the bank.
